jokes.text: What is it? What is it? It's tuna. I don't like tuna.
jokes.setup: Jeff offers Larry something without saying what it is
jokes.punchline: Larry's flat rejection of tuna with no elaboration
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - abrupt, matter-of-fact rejection. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: All right. The guy doesn't like tuna.
jokes.setup: Larry's blunt tuna rejection
jokes.punchline: Jeff's resigned acceptance and third-person commentary
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that shows Jeff's resignation to Larry's quirks.

jokes.text: That's my specialty, length.
jokes.setup: Julie compliments his movie's 'perfect length'
jokes.punchline: Larry turns it into an inadvertent sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Larry's oblivious sexual innuendo creates uncomfortable moment typical of the show.

jokes.text: The width, what about the width? There's some good width in that movie.
jokes.setup: Having made one innuendo about length
jokes.punchline: Larry doubles down with 'width' commentary
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the innuendo joke, making the awkwardness worse.

jokes.text: Johnny Carson used to say that. Fun stuff! He's really good at impersonations. What, do you have to be sarcastic? I could barely tell who you were doing!
jokes.setup: Someone does a Johnny Carson impression
jokes.punchline: Larry's harsh critique of the impression kills the moment
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to let people enjoy themselves, perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Personal reasons? She said, 'I've got personal reasons. I can't do it.' You can get away with anything. Nobody has to explain. They just say it's personal.
jokes.setup: Caterer cancels for 'personal reasons'
jokes.punchline: Larry's rant about 'personal reasons' as a universal excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry observation about social conventions - relatable, well-structured, quotable.

jokes.text: She said she liked it. She didn't like it. 'Enjoy' does not mean 'like.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl thinks Julie liked the movie
jokes.punchline: Larry's semantic analysis of 'enjoy' vs 'like'
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic parsing of language is economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: What is this compulsion to have people over your house and serve them food and talk to them? It's fun, it's a gathering, a party. What a strange thing.
jokes.setup: Cheryl defends having dinner parties
jokes.punchline: Larry finds the entire concept of hospitality bizarre
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's antisocial nature - finding basic human behavior mystifying.

jokes.text: People from Brooklyn don't walk around like that. We are in California now. You've gotta try a different way.
jokes.setup: Larry's antisocial behavior
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's geographic explanation for personality adjustment
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about regional personality differences.

jokes.text: I'd like to make a bet that I'm not close to having a good time.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry having fun at the party
jokes.punchline: Larry's confident prediction of his own misery
jokes.explanation: Great line - Larry betting against his own happiness is perfectly in character and quotable.

jokes.text: Blow job in the car. A blow job in the car. How about a side bet? Depends on where we're going.
jokes.setup: Making bets about the dinner party
jokes.punchline: Sexual bet followed by Cheryl's practical consideration of location
jokes.explanation: Mildly shocking sexual humor, somewhat cheap but gets reaction.

jokes.text: Until I moved to Los Angeles, I was never in the same room as a caterer. Ever, once. You can give me a Swanson's turkey dinner and I could care less.
jokes.setup: Discussion of caterers
jokes.punchline: Larry's down-to-earth preference for TV dinners
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's unpretentious food preferences despite wealth.

jokes.text: You never congratulated me on my new car. What, are you kidding? No, I'm not kidding. I was hurt by it.
jokes.setup: Jeff has something serious to discuss
jokes.punchline: The 'serious' issue is lack of car congratulations
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - builds up serious conversation only to reveal petty grievance. Well-structured.

jokes.text: It's not like having a kid. I didn't know that congratulations were in order when you get a new car.
jokes.setup: Jeff's hurt feelings about car congratulations
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical breakdown of what deserves congratulations
jokes.explanation: Larry's rational response to irrational social expectations.

jokes.text: What'd you pay for this? It's personal. Personal? What are you, insane? It's personal, everything's personal.
jokes.setup: Earlier rant about 'personal reasons' excuse
jokes.punchline: Jeff uses same excuse, Larry's frustrated recognition
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier 'personal reasons' observation, showing Larry's theory in action.

jokes.text: Aamco: double A, M-C-O. I didn't honk at you, you fucking asshole! It was the radio, Larry! What are you, nuts? Fucking idiot. Fucking asshole!
jokes.setup: Aamco commercial plays on radio with horn sound effects
jokes.punchline: Larry thinks another driver is honking and starts road rage incident
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup - commercial sound effects causing real-world chaos. High originality and impact.

jokes.text: 'Double A, beep, beep, M-C-O.' Everyone's heard that commercial.
jokes.setup: The Aamco incident
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated explanation of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to main joke, Jeff's frustration with Larry's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Larry, Aamco. 'Double A, beep, beep, M-C-O.' It sounded like his horn.
jokes.setup: Car making noise after accident
jokes.punchline: Larry still confused about commercial vs real horns
jokes.explanation: Continuation of Aamco joke, Larry still not getting it.

jokes.text: We are moving from, get this, from Covina to Downey. So I've got two lines up in Downey, and two lines going in Covina. It's just been... The books are split between Covina and Downey... and it's about a 35-, 40-minute drive between the two.
jokes.setup: Dinner party small talk
jokes.punchline: Excruciatingly boring business details delivered with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Boring dinner party conversation as comedy - realistic but not highly crafted.

jokes.text: Annie's a little girl, or an adult? Annie Get Your Gun. It's not Annie. Not little Annie? No, not with the dog. I was wondering, how's she gonna be on both?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Annie Get Your Gun musical
jokes.punchline: Larry confuses it with Little Orphan Annie, wonders about dog logistics
jokes.explanation: Larry's genuine confusion between two different Annies creates amusing misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Do you get paid every time they show that? Yeah. That's great. I was thinking the other day, how great it would be if Aamco paid me for transmissions that I fixed six years ago.
jokes.setup: Mike asks about Seinfeld residuals
jokes.punchline: Mike's analogy to his own work at Aamco
jokes.explanation: Clever analogy between entertainment residuals and blue-collar work. Well-constructed.

jokes.text: That's good, how you just did that. What? He's very good. You told him about the Aamco thing? No. Very funny. I work for Aamco. Very funny joke. I work for Aamco. No, I'm not kidding you. I do.
jokes.setup: Mike makes Aamco joke about residuals
jokes.punchline: Larry thinks it's a setup joke, but Mike actually works for Aamco
jokes.explanation: Brilliant coincidence - the Aamco incident leads to meeting an actual Aamco owner. High originality.

jokes.text: So I turned around, and I started yelling at the guy. He started yelling back. The guy got so pissed off he rammed us from behind and sped away.
jokes.setup: Explaining the Aamco commercial incident
jokes.punchline: The misunderstanding escalated to actual vehicular assault
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the earlier incident, showing consequences of Larry's misunderstanding.

jokes.text: In the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit. Amen. Bless us, Lord, and these thy gifts, which we are to receive from thy bounty, through Christ, our Lord. Amen.
jokes.setup: Dinner party with Larry, a secular Jew
jokes.punchline: Long, elaborate Catholic grace said in front of uncomfortable Larry
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Larry's discomfort during religious ritual, but not particularly crafted.

jokes.text: That was a good show, Friends. Did you have anything to do with that one? No, hell of a show. That's in reruns now, too, isn't it? Is it? I can't believe they get paid every time it comes on.
jokes.setup: Earlier discussion about Seinfeld residuals
jokes.punchline: Mike's continued fascination with TV residual payments
jokes.explanation: Callback to residuals topic, showing Mike's ongoing obsession with the concept.

jokes.text: Oh, boy! When's the next meeting of the Young Republicans' Club?
jokes.setup: Boring business talk about tax benefits
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic political jab at the conservative crowd
jokes.explanation: Larry's political sarcasm in response to business talk, economical and in character.

jokes.text: The next time you do one of these things, I want some Jews in the house. Okay, I'll make a note of that. I want some Cohens, some Bernsteins, some Goldsteins, a Schwartz, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry's discomfort at Christian dinner party
jokes.punchline: His specific request for Jewish guests by stereotypical surnames
jokes.explanation: Great Larry moment - specific, neurotic demand for religious diversity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: What about the guy who took my seat? What was that about? Yeah, why didn't you say something? He pushed me away because you said, 'Sit anywhere you want.'
jokes.setup: Mike sat in Larry's preferred chair
jokes.punchline: Larry's post-party grievance about seating arrangements
jokes.explanation: Larry's petty complaints after social events, very much in character.

jokes.text: How do you do that, again? 'The Father, Son, Holy Spirit.' What are you touching here? Your belly button? Some people go farther down, but most go to the chest.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about Catholic prayer gestures
jokes.punchline: His clueless questions about making the sign of the cross
jokes.explanation: Larry's cultural ignorance played for mild cringe, not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: And guess what? What? Somebody lost a little bet tonight. Somebody owes somebody...
jokes.setup: Earlier bet about Larry having a good time
jokes.punchline: Larry collecting on the sexual favor bet
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier sexual bet, mildly amusing resolution.

jokes.text: Can we please go back to Colgate? Why? I just don't like... There's no taste to that Indian thing.
jokes.setup: Morning routine discussion
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaints about ethnic toothpaste
jokes.explanation: Minor complaint in Larry's domestic life, not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Larry, Mike Duffy. The Aamco guy. Sorry for calling so early. I'm feeling weird about something that happened last night. I wanna talk to you about it.
jokes.setup: Vague, ominous message about feeling weird
jokes.punchline: Creates anxiety about what Larry might have done wrong
jokes.explanation: Sets up tension with vague message, typical Curb anxiety-inducing setup.

jokes.text: I hate messages like that. Either just say, 'Call me back,' or tell me why you're feeling weird. What could I have done to make him feel weird? I can't think of anything.
jokes.setup: Mike's vague message
jokes.punchline: Larry's neurotic analysis of proper message etiquette
jokes.explanation: Larry's neurotic overthinking of social communication, relatable observation.

jokes.text: Where's all the food from last night? Where are the leftovers? I don't know. Did you tell the caterer to take things home?
jokes.setup: Looking for leftover apple turnovers
jokes.punchline: Discovery that all the food is missing
jokes.explanation: Sets up the leftover theft plotline, good structure building tension.

jokes.text: This is stealing. I know it is. I mean, this is ridiculous. She didn't even ask anybody? I'm calling... This is very unprofessional, if you ask me. It's completely unprofessional.
jokes.setup: Discovering caterer took leftovers
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating outrage over food theft
jokes.explanation: Larry's proportional response to minor inconvenience, building the conflict.

jokes.text: We will cancel the check if we don't get the food back.
jokes.setup: Caterer stole leftovers
jokes.punchline: Nuclear option threat over leftover food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the leftover conflict to absurd proportions.

jokes.text: Survived the trip here? Where did you come from, the Sudan? What are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Caterer says food didn't survive the trip
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic geographic comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Larry sarcasm about absurd excuse for missing food.

jokes.text: You gave it to a homeless shelter? Yes, I did. And where is this homeless shelter? The Scott Mission on Fourth. So they eat this gourmet food? Catered food?
jokes.setup: Caterer claims she gave food to homeless shelter
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulity about homeless people eating gourmet catered food
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical questioning of the caterer's charitable story.

jokes.text: Sorry to interrupt, but I dropped the food off at Jeff's. You dropped the food off at Jeff Greene's?
jokes.setup: Caterer's homeless shelter story
jokes.punchline: Helper accidentally reveals food was actually given to Jeff
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that exposes the caterer's lie, well-structured dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Enjoying the chicken? I was gonna bring this by your house. What? Bring it by your house. I thought you wouldn't mind me having some.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers Jeff eating his stolen leftovers
jokes.punchline: Jeff's casual attitude about having Larry's stolen food
jokes.explanation: Good confrontation scene with Jeff's oblivious enjoyment of stolen food.

jokes.text: What a racket you've got going. What do you get? Ten percent of my salary, ten percent of my food?
jokes.setup: Jeff benefits from both the caterer recommendation and the stolen food
jokes.punchline: Larry's realization of Jeff's inadvertent scam
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about Jeff's unintentional double-dipping in Larry's resources.

jokes.text: What are the odds of an Aamco guy? There might be a problem with that.
jokes.setup: Discussing the coincidence of meeting the Aamco guy
jokes.punchline: Foreshadowing trouble with the relationship
jokes.explanation: Setup for final act conflict, connects back to main plot.

jokes.text: I got some good news and some bad news for you. The bad news is, you did some damage to the transmission. The good news is, my rash cleared up.
jokes.setup: Standard good news/bad news format about car repair
jokes.punchline: Good news is completely irrelevant personal information
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection in the good news/bad news format with irrelevant personal detail.

jokes.text: Actually, I was gonna apologize for sitting in your chair. That's what I wanted to talk about. You should've sat at the head of the table.
jokes.setup: Larry expects confrontation about Martha's Vineyard comment
jokes.punchline: Mike was actually worried about the chair situation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - reveals the real source of Mike's discomfort.

jokes.text: Ever eat over at that mission on Fourth Street? Do they ever serve anything like chicken l'orange or tuna tapenade? The only thing they serve is soup, and sometimes peanut butter.
jokes.setup: Larry investigating the caterer's homeless shelter story
jokes.punchline: Homeless man confirms the shelter doesn't serve gourmet food
jokes.explanation: Good callback that definitively exposes the caterer's lie through field research.

jokes.text: The only thing they serve is soup, and sometimes peanut butter. Soup and peanut butter.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about gourmet food at homeless shelter
jokes.punchline: Reality of basic soup and peanut butter vs. gourmet catering
jokes.explanation: Confirms the caterer's lie - homeless shelters don't serve gourmet catered food

jokes.text: Well... somebody lost a bet.
jokes.setup: End of episode after Larry's various social failures
jokes.punchline: Cheryl collecting on the bet about Larry having a good time
jokes.explanation: Bookend callback to opening bet, shows who ultimately won.
